  3. › Shetland Sheepdog vs Rough Collie

Shetland Sheepdog vs Rough Collie — Complete Comparison

Choosing between the Shetland Sheepdog and the Rough Collie comes down to lifestyle fit, living space, and the time you can commit to training and grooming. In terms of size, the Shetland Sheepdog is small while the Rough Collie is large, which affects exercise space requirements, feeding costs, and average lifespan. Both breeds have comparable exercise needs, though individual dogs will vary. Lifespan is also worth considering — Shetland Sheepdogs typically live 12–14 years, whereas Rough Collies average 14–16 years. Both breeds are well-suited to families with children.

Side-by-Side Comparison

Trait Shetland Sheepdog Rough Collie
Size Small Large
Weight 6.4–12 kg 18–34 kg
Height 33–41 cm 56–66 cm
Lifespan 12–14 yrs 14–16 yrs
Exercise Need
High (4/5)
Moderate (3/5)
Grooming Need
High (4/5)
High (4/5)
Shedding
High (4/5)
High (4/5)
Trainability
Very High (5/5)
Very High (5/5)
Good with Children ✓ ✓
Good with Dogs ✓ ✓
Apartment Friendly ✗ ✗

Bold green indicates the better result for that trait. Exercise, grooming and shedding: lower is easier to manage. Trainability and lifespan: higher is better.

Health Comparison

Understanding each breed's health profile helps owners budget for insurance, schedule appropriate screening, and make informed decisions before purchasing.

Shetland Sheepdog (5 conditions)

  • collie eye anomaly
  • hip dysplasia
  • progressive retinal atrophy
  • dermatomyositis
  • multiple drug sensitivity

Rough Collie (5 conditions)

  • collie eye anomaly
  • progressive retinal atrophy
  • multiple drug sensitivity
  • epilepsy
  • dermatomyositis

Conditions shared by both breeds: collie eye anomaly , progressive retinal atrophy , dermatomyositis , multiple drug sensitivity .

Which Breed Is Right for You?

Based on data across health, exercise, trainability, and temperament, here is how the two breeds compare across common owner priorities:

Best for families with children both Shetland Sheepdog and Rough Collie
Best for apartment living neither breed — both thrive with access to outdoor space
Best for active owners Shetland Sheepdog (exercise level 4/5)
Best for first-time owners Rough Collie
Longer average lifespan Rough Collie (14–16 yrs vs 12–14 yrs)

large · Herding · 14–16 yrs

Frequently Asked Questions

Which is bigger — a Shetland Sheepdog or a Rough Collie? +

The Rough Collie is the larger breed, typically weighing 18–34 kg compared to the Shetland Sheepdog's 6.4–12 kg.

Which breed lives longer — Shetland Sheepdog or Rough Collie? +

The Rough Collie generally has a longer lifespan (14–16 years) compared to the Shetland Sheepdog (12–14 years). Smaller breeds typically outlive larger ones, and individual genetics, diet, and preventive care all play significant roles.

Are Shetland Sheepdogs or Rough Collies easier to train? +

Both the Shetland Sheepdog and Rough Collie have comparable trainability (5/5). Consistent positive reinforcement methods work well for both breeds.

Which needs more grooming — Shetland Sheepdog or Rough Collie? +

Both breeds have similar grooming requirements (4/5). Regular brushing and routine coat maintenance apply equally to both.

Which breed is better for families — Shetland Sheepdog or Rough Collie? +

Both the Shetland Sheepdog and Rough Collie are generally considered good family dogs and are typically patient and gentle with children. As always, early socialisation and teaching children how to interact respectfully with dogs are essential regardless of breed.

Do Shetland Sheepdogs and Rough Collies have the same health problems? +

Some health conditions are shared between both breeds, including collie eye anomaly, progressive retinal atrophy, dermatomyositis. The Shetland Sheepdog has 5 documented conditions overall and the Rough Collie has 5. Pet insurance is strongly recommended for both.
